title: AITP Conformance Fixture (metadata block)
description: Validates the leading metadata block every fixture under schemas/conformance/ MUST carry. Scenario content (`input`, `expected`, `tags`, etc.) is fixture-shape-specific and is not validated here; this schema enforces only the metadata contract that a v0.1 conformance runner relies on.

required_for_v0_1.description: Whether a v0.1 implementation MUST pass this fixture. Always false when status != 'core'.

feature.description: Opt-in feature flag for non-core fixtures (null for core).

dynamic.description: Optional. When true, the fixture carries placeholder values in time-sensitive fields that cannot be statically minted; a conformance runner MUST regenerate them with the pinned KAT keypairs before execution. See schemas/conformance/README.md 'Dynamic fixtures'. Absent means the fixture is statically mintable.

dynamic_fields.description: Optional. Names of the fields a runner MUST regenerate when `dynamic` is true (e.g. issued_at, expires_at, signature, timestamp, nonce, nonce_echo, pop_signature).

$comment.description: Optional human-readable note. Ignored by conformance runners.

expected.description: Per-fixture expected outcome. Shape is fixture-shape-specific; this schema validates only the optional, cross-cutting `side_effects` block. All other fields (outcome, error_code, grants, rationale, etc.) are scenario-defined and NOT validated here.

side_effects.description: Optional assertion on side effects the implementation MUST or MUST NOT produce while evaluating the fixture. Conformance runners that instrument these side effects MUST assert the value matches; runners that do not instrument a given side effect MUST report SKIP for that assertion (NOT silent pass). Existing fixtures without `side_effects` are unaffected — this is an additive field.

revocation_lookup_called.description: Whether the implementation called the network revocation source (e.g. `ListRevoked` fetch) during fixture evaluation. Used by rev-004 to pin the RFC-AITP-0008 §3.3 ordering requirement: TCT signature verification MUST precede any network revocation lookup.

network_fetch_called.description: Whether the implementation made ANY outbound network fetch (HTTP, DNS, etc.) during fixture evaluation. Broader than `revocation_lookup_called`; useful for DoS-amplification assertions where any outbound traffic is the failure mode.

pop_challenge_issued.description: Whether the verifier issued a `pop_challenge` during fixture evaluation. Used by tct-007 to assert PoP enforcement for grants marked as requiring PoP (RFC-AITP-0005 §6.2).

capability_authorized.description: Whether the verifier authorized a capability invocation during fixture evaluation. Used by tct-007 to assert that an invocation against a PoP-required grant MUST NOT be authorized in the absence of a valid `pop_response`.
